answer:
**In Allama Iqbal's *Bang-e-Dara* (The Call of the Marching Bell, 1924)—divided into three parts in this corpus—the Arabic/Urdu term *mu'min* (مؤمن, "believer" or "faithful one") appears explicitly only once, in Part 1, Chapter 38 ("Sayed Ki Loh-e-Turbat," or "The Epitaph of the Sayed").**[^1]

**Iqbal presents the *banda-e momin* ("true Believer" or "Believer's servant" in a poetic sense, referring to the ideal faithful individual) as a figure of moral purity and fearless resolve:**  
> A true Believer's heart is free of hypocrisy and fear;  
> It stands unflinching when the force of worldly power is near.[^2]

This stanza, voiced through an epitaph on a Sayed's (descendant of the Prophet) tombstone, contrasts the *momin*'s inner strength with superficial piety. The *momin* acts with noble intent (*nek hai neeyat*), undeterred by consequences (*to kya parwa tujhe*), embodying boldness (*be-baak*) before oppressive authority (*quwwat-e farmaan-rawa*). It fits Iqbal's broader motif of spiritual revival, urging poets and statesmen to awaken slumbering hearts without fear.[^3]

**No other instances of *mu'min*, *momin*, *mūmin*, or close English equivalents ("true believer," "faithful one," "Believer") appear across *Bang-e-Dara*'s ~100 chapters (Parts 1–3).** Related ideals surface indirectly, e.g., in Part 3, Chapter 10 ("Har nafas Iqbal tera aah mein mastoor hai," titled "The Muslim" in translation), which exalts the Muslim as a "radiant star" guarding cosmic truth, fearless amid sorrow.[^4] But *mu'min* itself is confined to this single, vivid portrait of dynamic faith.

Iqbal's full philosophy of the *momin* as a self-realizing, world-shaping force develops more in later prose like *The Reconstruction of Religious Thought in Islam* (not in this corpus). Here in *Bang-e-Dara*, it emerges as a call to fearless authenticity.

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 38 "Sayed Ki Loh-e-Turbat" ([translation](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/)).

[^2]: Ibid., [p-c379c6](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/#p-c379c6). Transliteration: "Banda-e Momin ka dil beem-o-riya se paak hai / Quwwat-e farmaan-rawa ke saamne be-baak hai."

[^3]: Ibid., [p-0cf5a4](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/#p-0cf5a4) and [p-2e3845](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/38-sayed-ki-loh-e-turbat/translation/#p-2e3845).

[^4]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 10 ([translation](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/10-har-nafas-iqbal-tera-aah-mein-mastoor-hai/translation/)).
